title: Cyathocalyx carinatus
intro: Cyathocalyx carinatus, also known as the Borneo wood fern, is a species of fern native to Borneo. This plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body. The bioactive constituents of Cyathocalyx carinatus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Cyathocalyx carinatus is classified within the family Annonaceae, commonly known as the custard apple family. This family is a part of the order Magnoliales, which consists of flowering plants with distinct characteristics. The order Magnoliales is a subset of the clade Magnoliidae, a group of flowering plants that are often associated with ancient lineages. The clade Magnoliidae is a part of the larger clade Chloranthoidae, which includes plants with unique characteristics such as flowers with three petals and sepals. These classifications are based on molecular phylogenetic studies that have shed light on the evolutionary relationships among various plant species.
uses: Cyathocalyx carinatus is a plant species known for its medicinal properties, with various studies and traditional practices suggesting its potential in treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The plant's extracts have been used to alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Additionally, it is believed to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in reducing swelling and pain.
